text: - Hello. - Yeah, I can hop you up. - Good morning. - Hello. - My name is Cassie. I'm calling because your Medicare Member Profile hasn't been transferred into Medicare's national database for 2026 yet. When that transfer is missing, it can cause delays in your coverage approvals. I can verify it real quick, so you do have both Part A and Part B, right? - Yes, I do. - Yes, I do. - You do qualify forwarding you to our supervisor now. Please hold. - Whatever. [BEEP] - Thank you so much for holding. This is Sun Murphy. How are you? - Fine, thank you. - And what you say is that calls are a gas to about Medicare benefits, you do have Part A and BF Medicare, right? - Yes, I do. - Great. - Well, the good news for you, this is a special enrollment period, 2026, and all Medicare benefits are just in it now, where you will be qualified to get more coverage on your dental, vision, food allowance, and cashback. Are you just on Medicare or you also have Medicaid? - I don't have Medicaid. - And Garforbid, do you have any chronic illness like diabetes or heart issues and COPD? - No, I'm more on prevention than it is in the middle of what I'm going through. - I say, do you have any chronic illness like diabetes, heart issues? - Yes, diabetes, yeah. - Okay, so you don't have a first card? - I just have three cancers. I just got three cancers last year. - Okay, now you don't have a flex card for the healthy food, groceries, and over-the-counter items coverage. - You don't have a spendable card? - No, I don't. - Yeah, the good news for you, Medicare, and also the people who have diabetes or any chronic illness, they are always going to get help on the food, okay? It means you're always going to get a food allowance card, and also you're always going to get more help on the dental and vision, and you can get more help on the groceries as well. And I believe so, you will be not paying any co-pays on your doctor visits and your specialists, right? - No, I don't. - And do you pay any co-pays on your prescription drugs, your medications? - Some of them. Some of them. - Some of them? Okay, no worries. We can also help you with that. And who is your credit insurance provider? It's United Healthcare, Humana, Edna? - United Healthcare. - Great, so you're on the right place, and that makes the job easier to help you. We represent United Healthcare and all the major carriers as well. - What is yours up, Kurtzer? - 80620. - 80620, state of Colorado? - Evans, Colorado. - Got it, sir. Let me ask you a few good, simple qualifying questions just to make you eligible to get your more benefits and help you to save your money. Do you make all of your own healthcare decisions? - Yes, I do. - And do you have any military insurance like TriCare, VA, any veteran medical? - No, no, no. - And do you have any insurance for the job, previous employer? - No, I don't. - And do you live in an own place, or are you living in a nursing hall by a sister living any kind of facility? - No, I live in my own home. - All right, sir. And where is your age? How old are you? - 80. - 80, okay. And just to address you properly, how you say your first and last name, what's your first name? - Well, you're calling me, you know all my information, you should know that. - Yes, I do know that. I just want to reconfirm your name, and then I will connect you with your area Medicare specialists in the Colorado Department, so they give you all that. - And you call me, so you got to know my name. - Sir, I don't need any kind of your other person formation. I'm the verification officer. My job is to confirm your name, your zip code, your database, and then I will connect your call to an agent. There's your area Medicare specialists, and they represent United Healthcare, so they'll be helping you to get your benefits. - So I just, I mean, I talked to my agent, she says, I don't know what I think. - Because, you know... - But you know... - But you know... - Why would she write to me? - I don't know when you talk with them, like I say, Medicare just updated the benefits, and every person, if you have a friend, any family member, so if they have any chronic illness, people are getting like a food card, you ask them, they will let you, you know, you're also eligible to get a food benefit card, because of your chronic illness, you have diabetes. So I wait this my time to tell you a lie, you know, I was just trying to help you to get your benefits. - And if you look at the stats now, 41%... - Okay, my name is Jesus Malonaro. - Okay, I'm so sorry, what's your first name? - Jesus. - Okay, what's your last name? - Malonaro. - How do you spell your last name? - M-A-L-D-O-N-A-D-O. - Okay, M-A-L-D-O-A-M-A-L-D-O-N-A-D-O. - I got it, first name is J-E-S-U-S, right? - Yes. - Thank you, sir, thank you so much for coming back. Now that's all done from my side, just go ahead and quickly connect you into the Colorado department with your area agent, because they do represent all the carriers in your area, UnitedHealthcare is one of them, okay? So they will take a little and let you know that how much money you always will on the food card, and they will send you the paperwork and the food card as well, and they help you on more dental invasion. Do you need more help on that, dental vision? - Who doesn't? - Yes, I do. - Okay, what kind of dental works you need? Like cleaning, fillings, dirt canal? - No, just cleaning. - Just cleaning and do you wear prescription glasses, or just from waiting one? - Just bring your glasses. - Got it. Mr. Milano, that's all done from my side. I will just quickly bring an agent, so they help you on their benefits. They also send you the paperwork as well. Do I have your permission to connect this call to an agent, even if your number is on any federal or state do not call it? Do I have your permission to connect your call? Please say yes. - It's problematic, and you know it's popular. - I have your permission to connect your call to an agent.
